footer.signature: designed & built by yunus emre korkmaz

intro.marqueeText: minimalism, ui/ux design, perfectionism, refined aesthetics,

welcome.name: [yunus emre korkmaz]
welcome.description: I design and build modern, intentional and motion-driven digital experiences.

aboutHome.mainText: I merge design clarity with engineering precision to craft thoughtful digital products.

principles.title: [principles]
list: details matter, but flow matters more

manifest.title: [ mental architecture ]
manifest.desc: interfaces are not made of pixels, but of rational decisions and controlled chaos. I don't just write code here; I ruthlessly annihilate possibilities, forcing the system into a single absolute reality. This is the cost of order.

aesthetic.label: aesthetics
aesthetic.text: beauty is not decoration; it is the ruthless amputation of excess. The void is the most strategic architecture left for the system to breathe. Only the necessary survives.

entropy.label: entropy
entropy.text: flawless order defies the nature of the universe. To sustain an interface is to relentlessly inject rational energy into a structure that constantly tends toward decay.

rationality.label: rationality
rationality.text: every rational decision is the murder of infinite other possibilities. Making a choice is not freedom; it is the art of constraining the system and condemning it to a single outcome.

contactHome.subtext: I’m open to collaborations, freelance work and product-focused projects.

heroAbout.title: merging design thinking with frontend engineering.

aboutmePage.title: [who am i]
aboutmePage.bio_1: i'm yunus emre korkmaz — a hybrid frontend developer & UI/UX designer with experience creating scalable, accessible, and visually refined digital products.
aboutmePage.bio_2: I obsess over micro-interactions and clarity, so products feel effortless from first click to last scroll.

whatIDo.title: [what i do]
whatIDo.desc_1: i design and build digital interfaces with structure, intention, and expression.
whatIDo.desc_2: my work focuses on clarity, usability, and motion-led visual identity.

discover.title: [discover me]
discover.subtitle: express yourself freely. draw the shapes below to uncover how i work and what drives my creative process.

cards.title: the big picture
cards.description: i don't just fit pieces together; i solve the whole puzzle. bridging the gap between user needs, business goals, and technical reality.

cards.instruction: draw a pen inside the box
cards.title: design engineering
cards.description: writing code with a designer’s eye. i translate creative concepts into pixel-perfect interfaces without losing clarity in translation.

cards.title: shipped & solid
cards.description: it’s not done until it feels right. building accessible, performant, and polished experiences that users can trust.

projects.header_desc: a selection of professional projects i've designed and developed over the past years. from municipal platforms to startup products, each project focuses on clean UI, accessibility, and meaningful interaction.

defaults.overview: Modern, scalable and accessible interfaces designed for real users.
defaults.conclusion: this project allowed me to combine clean ui principles, fast development practices and modern motion design—while solving real-world problems for real users.

white_on_white.question: what does ‘white on white’ mean?
white_on_white.footer: in design, light is the father of form. Hiding shadows is easy; mastering light requires craft. Pure white is a screen's highest energy state. I do not hide the energy; I harness it through architecture.

dichotomy.left_annotation_desc: Calculations, boundaries, and architecture. As flawless as a component's lifecycle, an enemy of entropy. It rejects emotion, accepting only data. Karl Friston's Free Energy Principle operates here: No surprises, no errors.
dichotomy.right_annotation_desc: The deep isolation in Balıkesir. The raw anger following a lost hand on the chessboard. The irregular motivation that moves the body pointlessly every day. Flawed, unpredictable, but undeniably human.

cost_of_order.title: [ the entropy of mind ]
cost_of_order.desc: Karl Friston was right. The mind is a machine that constantly expends energy to minimize surprise and entropy. Just like the systems I build. Order is not natural. Order is a paid-for effort. A flawless code architecture or a rational decision is a temporary war waged against the natural chaos of the universe. The moment you cut the energy, the system returns to its natural state.

observer.title: [ the observer's tyranny ]
observer.desc: the system holds all errors and all truths simultaneously until I observe it. reality is a heap of possibilities pruned by the attention of a rational observer. i don't write code; i force probabilities into a single rational output.
lines: attention is the purest form of energy.

choice.title: [ the gravity of 'yes' ]
choice.desc: rationality is not freedom; it is a form of constraint. to choose one path is to annihilate all others. choice is a rational mass; it requires effort to carry.

asymmetry.title: [ the asymmetry of loss ]
asymmetry.desc: daniel kahneman was right. the mind is programmed for the trauma of loss rather than the thrill of gain. building a new structure takes time; destroying it forces the entire system to face gravity. losing is not the opposite of gaining, but its mutation.

protocol.title: [ protocol_00: handshake ]
protocol.desc: You have seen the architecture of my mind and the price I pay to build a system. Order is not natural; it can only be sustained by ruthless rationality. If your project requires this level of gravity and structural effort, open the communication channel. Otherwise, you may exit the system.

contactPage.sidebar_title: please contact me to set up an online meeting or ask any questions you have.
